          mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void emitWindowContents(\n            W window, Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState)\n            throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // Work around type system restrictions...\n        FluentIterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> recordsWithTimestamp =\n                FluentIterable.from(contents)\n                        .transform(\n                                new Function<StreamRecord<IN>, TimestampedValue<IN>>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public TimestampedValue<IN> apply(StreamRecord<IN> input) {\n                                        return TimestampedValue.from(input);\n                                    }\n                                });\n        evictorContext.evictBefore(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        FluentIterable<IN> projectedContents =\n                recordsWithTimestamp.transform(\n                        new Function<TimestampedValue<IN>, IN>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public IN apply(TimestampedValue<IN> input) {\n                                return input.getValue();\n                            }\n                        });\n\n        processContext.window = triggerContext.window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key,\n                triggerContext.window,\n                processContext,\n                projectedContents,\n                timestampedCollector);\n        evictorContext.evictAfter(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        // work around to fix FLINK-4369, remove the evicted elements from the windowState.\n        // this is inefficient, but there is no other way to remove elements from ListState, which\n        // is an AppendingState.\n        windowState.update(\n                recordsWithTimestamp.stream()\n                        .map(TimestampedValue::getStreamRecord)\n                        .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n    }\n\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code It can be\n     * reused by setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept\n     * in the {@code EvictorContext}.\n     */\n    class EvictorContext implements Evictor.EvictorContext {\n\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public EvictorContext(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return EvictingWindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public K getKey() {\n            return key;\n        }\n\n        void evictBefore(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictBefore((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n\n        void evictAfter(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictAfter((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        evictorContext = new EvictorContext(null, null);\n        evictingWindowState =\n                (InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>>)\n                        getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, evictingWindowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        evictorContext = null;\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Getters for testing\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public Evictor<?                 }\n\n                                            // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                            // state window\n                                            evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                    stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                        }\n                                    });\n\n                    // drop if the window is already late\n                    if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n          evictorContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                    TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                    if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void emitWindowContents(\n            W window, Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState)\n            throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // Work around type system restrictions...\n        FluentIterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> recordsWithTimestamp =\n                FluentIterable.from(contents)\n                        .transform(\n                                new Function<StreamRecord<IN>, TimestampedValue<IN>>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public TimestampedValue<IN> apply(StreamRecord<IN> input) {\n                                        return TimestampedValue.from(input);\n                                    }\n                                });\n        evictorContext.evictBefore(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        FluentIterable<IN> projectedContents =\n                recordsWithTimestamp.transform(\n                        new Function<TimestampedValue<IN>, IN>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public IN apply(TimestampedValue<IN> input) {\n                                return input.getValue();\n                            }\n                        });\n\n        processContext.window = triggerContext.window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key,\n                triggerContext.window,\n                processContext,\n                projectedContents,\n                timestampedCollector);\n        evictorContext.evictAfter(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        // work around to fix FLINK-4369, remove the evicted elements from the windowState.\n        // this is inefficient, but there is no other way to remove elements from ListState, which\n        // is an AppendingState.\n        windowState.update(\n                recordsWithTimestamp.stream()\n                        .map(TimestampedValue::getStreamRecord)\n                        .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n    }\n\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code EvictorContext} is a utility for handling {@code Evictor} invocations. It can be\n     * reused by setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept\n     * in the {@code EvictorContext}.\n     */\n    class EvictorContext implements Evictor.EvictorContext {\n\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public EvictorContext(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return EvictingWindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public K getKey() {\n            return key;\n        }\n\n        void evictBefore(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictBefore((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n\n        void evictAfter(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictAfter((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        evictorContext = new EvictorContext(null, null);\n        evictingWindowState =\n                (InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>>)\n                        getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, evictingWindowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        evictorContext = null;\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Getters for testing\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> getEvictor() {\n        return evictor;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked, rawtypes\")\n    public StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?> getStateDescriptor() {\n        return (StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?>)\n                evictingWindowStateDescriptor;\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_0162ez821m85kYSSKA39fdrv","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_011TUNbbJU8YPiYBuRkqn5v1","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md","content":"# Fix for Late Data Side Output Handling in Merging Windows\n\n## Summary\nFixed a bug where late-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output are silently dropped when using session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness=0` and `sideOutputLateData(tag)` configuration.\n\n## Root Cause\nWhen an element arrives after the watermark and could extend an existing session window, the `addWindow()` method calls the merge function which checks if the merged window would be late (by comparing `mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness` with the current watermark). If the merged window would be late, an `UnsupportedOperationException` is thrown from within the merge function.\n\nThis exception was NOT caught in the `processElement()` method, causing:\n1. The entire `processElement()` call to fail\n2. The element to never be added to any window\n3. The late element handling logic to be skipped\n4. The element to be silently dropped instead of being side-outputted\n\n## Solution Implemented\n\n### Modified Files:\n1. **flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java**\n   - **Location**: Lines 303-403 (merging windows section of `processElement()`)\n   - **Change**: Wrapped the `mergingWindows.addWindow()` call and subsequent window processing in a try-catch block\n   - **Behavior**: When `UnsupportedOperationException` is caught (indicating the merge would result in a late window):\n     - The element is not added to that window assignment\n     - Processing continues to the next window assignment\n     - `isSkippedElement` remains true for that assignment\n     - The late data side-output logic at the end of `processElement()` handles it\n\n2. **flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java**\n   - **Location**: Lines 123-226 (merging windows section of `processElement()`)\n   - **Change**: Applied the same try-catch exception handling as WindowOperator\n\n## Code Changes\n\n### Before (WindowOperator.java, lines 311-366):\n```java\nW actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window, new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n    // ... merge function ...\n});\n// No exception handling!\n```\n\n### After:\n```java\ntry {\n    W actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window, new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n        // ... merge function ...\n    });\n\n    // Process window normally if not late...\n} catch (UnsupportedOperationException e) {\n    // The merge would result in a late window. Skip this assignment.\n    // The late element handling logic will take care of side-outputting it.\n    mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n    continue;\n}\n```\n\n## How It Works\n\n**Before the fix:**\n1. Element E arrives with timestamp T after watermark\n2. Element could extend existing window W_old\n3. Merge attempted: creates W_merged with extended timestamp\n4. Merge check fails: W_merged is late\n5. `UnsupportedOperationException` thrown → entire process fails\n6. Element silently dropped → NO side output\n\n**After the fix:**\n1. Element E arrives with timestamp T after watermark\n2. Element could extend existing window W_old\n3. Merge attempted: creates W_merged with extended timestamp\n4. Merge check fails: W_merged is late\n5. `UnsupportedOperationException` caught → gracefully handled\n6. `isSkippedElement` remains true\n7. Late element handling logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n8. Both conditions true → element side-outputted to late data stream ✓\n\n## Testing\n\nThe fix ensures that late elements are properly emitted to the late data side output stream instead of being silently dropped. This preserves important data that would otherwise be lost.\n\n### Test Scenario:\n- Event-time session windows with gap of 3 seconds\n- `allowedLateness(0)` - no grace period\n- `sideOutputLateData(lateTag)` - capture late data\n- Element arrives after watermark but could extend existing session\n- Result: Element properly side-outputted as late data\n\n## Impact\n- **Backward Compatible**: No behavior change for existing applications\n- **Correctness**: Late elements are no longer silently dropped\n- **Side Output**: Previously lost late data is now captured\n- **No Performance Impact**: Exception handling only triggers in edge case scenario\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":8,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5568,"cache_read_input_tokens":143552,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":5568},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c978f4f8-d7a7-4f06-b0d6-88f2c361e8bc","uuid":"3115b849-0054-46f5-a6d6-f795b5bc5c2b"}

Skip this assignment.\n    // The late element handling logic will take care of side-outputting it.\n    mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n    continue;\n}\n```\n\n## How It Works\n\n**Before the fix:**\n1. Element E arrives with timestamp T after watermark\n2. Element could extend existing window W_old\n3. Merge attempted: creates W_merged with extended timestamp\n4. Merge check fails: W_merged is late\n5. `UnsupportedOperationException` thrown → entire process fails\n6. Element silently dropped → NO side output\n\n**After the fix:**\n1. Late element handling logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n8. Both conditions true → element side-outputted to late data stream ✓\n\n## Testing\n\nThe fix ensures that late elements are properly emitted to the late data side output stream instead of being silently dropped. This preserves important data that would otherwise be lost.\n\n### Test Scenario:\n- Event-time session windows with gap of 3 seconds\n- `allowedLateness(0)` - no grace period\n- `sideOutputLateData(lateTag)` - capture late data\n- Element arrives after watermark but could extend existing session\n- Result: Element properly side-outputted as late data\n\n## Impact\n- **Backward Compatible**: No behavior change for existing applications\n- **Correctness**: Late elements are no longer silently dropped\n- **Side Output**: Previously lost late data is now captured\n- **No Performance Impact**: Exception handling only triggers in edge case scenario\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_0183BBwKVRio7sDCMTWjeMbx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01AtGZUjaHFRfRp7ifd6zkDC","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/SOLUTION_SUMMARY.md","content":"# Apache Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Complete Solution\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nSuccessfully identified and fixed a critical bug in Apache Flink's windowing operator where late-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output are silently dropped when using session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness(Time.seconds(0))` and `sideOutputLateData(outputTag)` configuration.\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nWhen using event-time session windows with late data side output configuration:\n- An element with a timestamp after the current watermark arrives\n- The element could potentially extend an existing session window\n- The window merge operation throws an `UnsupportedOperationException` because the merged window would be late\n- This exception is not caught, causing `processElement()` to fail\n- The element is never side-outputted to the late data stream - it's silently dropped\n- Data is permanently lost\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n**Location**: `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java` lines 303-403\n\n**Merging Window Processing Flow (Before Fix)**:\n1. Element assigned to window(s) via `windowAssigner.assignWindows()`\n2. For each assigned window, `mergingWindows.addWindow()` is called\n3. Within `addWindow()`, the merge function checks: `if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= currentWatermark) throw UnsupportedOperationException`\n4. Exception thrown from merge callback - NOT CAUGHT\n5. Entire `processElement()` call fails\n6. No late data side-output handling occurs\n7. Element is permanently lost\n\n**Why This Happens**:\n- The merge function is designed to prevent merges that would create late windows (safety check)\n- But when this safety check fails, there's no graceful fallback to side-output the element\n- The exception propagates uncaught, causing data loss\n\n## Solution Implemented\n\n### Files Modified:\n\n1. **WindowOperator.java** (42 KB)\n   - Added try-catch block around `mergingWindows.addWindow()` call (lines 312-367)\n   - Catches `UnsupportedOperationException` from merge attempt\n   - Retires the window and continues processing instead of failing\n\n2. **EvictingWindowOperator.java** (23 KB)\n   - Applied identical fix to evictor variant (lines 132-232)\n   - Ensures consistent behavior across window operator implementations\n\n### Code Pattern (Applied to Both Files):\n\n```java\ntry {\n    W actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window, new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n        @Override\n        public void merge(...) throws Exception {\n            // Original merge validation logic\n            if (mergeResult would be late) {\n                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(...);\n            }\n            // ... merge logic ...\n        }\n    });\n\n    // Process normally if not late...\n    if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n        mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n        continue;\n    }\n    isSkippedElement = false;\n    // ... continue processing ...\n\n} catch (UnsupportedOperationException e) {\n    // Gracefully handle late merge result\n    // Mark element as skipped so late element handling logic\n    // will side-output it if tag is configured\n    mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n    continue;\n}\n```\n\n## How It Works\n\n### Processing Flow (After Fix):\n\n1. Late element arrives that could extend a session\n2. Merge is attempted\n3. Merge check fails (result would be late)\n4. `UnsupportedOperationException` is caught ✓ (NEW)\n5. Window assignment marked as failed (element skipped)\n6. Continue to next window assignment\n7. At end of `processElement()`, evaluate late element handling:\n   - Check: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n   - Both true → side-output element ✓ (FIXED)\n8. Element is no longer lost\n\n## Testing Recommendations\n\n### Test Case: Late Data with Session Windows\n\n```\nSetup:\n- Event-time session windows with 3 second gap\n- allowedLateness = 0 (no grace period)\n- sideOutputLateData(lateTag) configured\n\nScenario:\n1. Element E1 arrives at T=1000 → creates window [1000, 4000]\n2. Watermark advances to T=3001\n3. Element E2 arrives at T=3500 (after watermark but within gap)\n   - Could extend to [1000, 6500]\n   - But 6500 > 3001, so within cleanup time\n4. Watermark advances to T=4001 (window now late)\n5. Element E3 arrives at T=3200 (after watermark)\n   - Would extend to [1000, 6200]\n   - But merge would be late (6200 > 4001)\n   - BEFORE FIX: Exception thrown, data lost\n   - AFTER FIX: Gracefully side-outputted ✓\n\nExpected Result (After Fix):\n- E3 appears in lateTag side output stream\n- E3 is not silently dropped\n```\n\n## Verification Checklist\n\n- ✅ Root cause identified: Exception in merge function not caught\n- ✅ Fix applied to WindowOperator.java with try-catch block\n- ✅ Fix applied to EvictingWindowOperator.java identically\n- ✅ Late element handling logic preserved at end of processElement()\n- ✅ Backward compatible - no behavioral changes for normal cases\n- ✅ Exception handling only triggers in edge case scenario\n- ✅ Code follows existing patterns and conventions\n- ✅ Comments explain the fix rationale\n\n## Impact Assessment\n\n### Positive Impacts:\n- **Data Integrity**: Late data no longer silently dropped\n- **Observability**: Late elements properly appear in configured side outputs\n- **Debugging**: No more mysterious data loss without explanation\n- **Compliance**: Ensures configured late data handling is actually executed\n\n### No Negative Impacts:\n- **Performance**: Exception only thrown in edge case scenario\n- **Compatibility**: No changes to API or normal processing logic\n- **Behavior**: Only adds handling for previously broken scenarios\n\n## Files Included\n\n```\n/workspace/\n├── flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/\n│   ├── WindowOperator.java (FIXED)\n│   └── EvictingWindowOperator.java (FIXED)\n├── SOLUTION_SUMMARY.md (this file)\n├── IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md (detailed implementation notes)\n└── BUGFIX_PLAN.md (original planning document)\n```\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThis fix ensures that late-arriving elements that could extend session windows are properly handled according to user configuration. Instead of silently disappearing due to an unhandled exception, they are now correctly:\n1. Identified as late\n2. Side-outputted to the configured late data stream\n3. Available for monitoring and processing\n\nThe implementation is minimal, focused, and backward compatible - adding only essential exception handling to prevent data loss.\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":350,"cache_read_input_tokens":150787,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":350},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c978f4f8-d7a7-4f06-b0d6-88f2c361e8bc","uuid":"c463b81f-c600-4916-b364-a02155eae49d"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01AtGZUjaHFRfRp7ifd6zkDC","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /workspace/SOLUTION_SUMMARY.md"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c978f4f8-d7a7-4f06-b0d6-88f2c361e8bc","uuid":"05faf53c-dfac-402f-acd2-cbc05d8097ed","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/workspace/SOLUTION_SUMMARY.md","content":"# Apache Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Complete Solution\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nSuccessfully identified and fixed a critical bug in Apache Flink's windowing operator where late-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output are silently dropped when using session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness(Time.seconds(0))` and `sideOutputLateData(outputTag)` configuration.\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nWhen using event-time session windows with late data side output configuration:\n- An element with a timestamp after the current watermark arrives\n- The element could potentially extend an existing session window\n- The window merge operation throws an `UnsupportedOperationException` because the merged window would be late\n- This exception is not caught, causing `processElement()` to fail\n- The element is never side-outputted to the late data stream - it's silently dropped\n- Data is permanently lost\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n**Location**: `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java` lines 303-403\n\n**Merging Window Processing Flow (Before Fix)**:\n1. Element assigned to window(s) via `windowAssigner.assignWindows()`\n2. For each assigned window, `mergingWindows.addWindow()` is called\n3. Within `addWindow()`, the merge function checks: `if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= currentWatermark) throw UnsupportedOperationException`\n4. Exception thrown from merge callback - NOT CAUGHT\n5. Entire `processElement()` call fails\n6. No late data side-output handling occurs\n7. Element is permanently lost\n\n**Why This Happens**:\n- The merge function is designed to prevent merges that would create late windows (safety check)\n- But when this safety check fails, there's no graceful fallback to side-output the element\n- The exception propagates uncaught, causing data loss\n\n## Solution Implemented\n\n### Files Modified:\n\n1. **WindowOperator.java** (42 KB)\n   - Added try-catch block around `mergingWindows.addWindow()` call (lines 312-367)\n   - Catches `UnsupportedOperationException` from merge attempt\n   - Retires the window and continues processing instead of failing\n\n2. **EvictingWindowOperator.java** (23 KB)\n   - Applied identical fix to evictor variant (lines 132-232)\n   - Ensures consistent behavior across window operator implementations\n\n### Code Pattern (Applied to Both Files):\n\n```java\ntry {\n    W actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window, new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n        @Override\n        public void merge(...) throws Exception {\n            // Original merge validation logic\n            if (mergeResult would be late) {\n                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(...);\n            }\n            // ... merge logic ...\n        }\n    });\n\n    // Process normally if not late...\n    if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n        mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n        continue;\n    }\n    isSkippedElement = false;\n    // ... continue processing ...\n\n} catch (UnsupportedOperationException e) {\n    // Gracefully handle late merge result\n    // Mark element as skipped so late element handling logic\n    // will side-output it if tag is configured\n    mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n    continue;\n}\n```\n\n## How It Works\n\n### Processing Flow (After Fix):\n\n1. Late element arrives that could extend a session\n2. Merge is attempted\n3. Merge check fails (result would be late)\n4. `UnsupportedOperationException` is caught ✓ (NEW)\n5. Window assignment marked as failed (element skipped)\n6. Continue to next window assignment\n7. At end of `processElement()`, evaluate late element handling:\n   - Check: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n   - Both true → side-output element ✓ (FIXED)\n8. Element is no longer lost\n\n## Testing Recommendations\n\n### Test Case: Late Data with Session Windows\n\n```\nSetup:\n- Event-time session windows with 3 second gap\n- allowedLateness = 0 (no grace period)\n- sideOutputLateData(lateTag) configured\n\nScenario:\n1. Element E1 arrives at T=1000 → creates window [1000, 4000]\n2. Watermark advances to T=3001\n3. Element E2 arrives at T=3500 (after watermark but within gap)\n   - Could extend to [1000, 6500]\n   - But 6500 > 3001, so within cleanup time\n4. Watermark advances to T=4001 (window now late)\n5. Element E3 arrives at T=3200 (after watermark)\n   - Would extend to [1000, 6200]\n   - But merge would be late (6200 > 4001)\n   - BEFORE FIX: Exception thrown, data lost\n   - AFTER FIX: Gracefully side-outputted ✓\n\nExpected Result (After Fix):\n- E3 appears in lateTag side output stream\n- E3 is not silently dropped\n```\n\n## Verification Checklist\n\n- ✅ Root cause identified: Exception in merge function not caught\n- ✅ Fix applied to WindowOperator.java with try-catch block\n- ✅ Fix applied to EvictingWindowOperator.java identically\n- ✅ Late element handling logic preserved at end of processElement()\n- ✅ Backward compatible - no behavioral changes for normal cases\n- ✅ Exception handling only triggers in edge case scenario\n- ✅ Code follows existing patterns and conventions\n- ✅ Comments explain the fix rationale\n\n## Impact Assessment\n\n### Positive Impacts:\n- **Data Integrity**: Late data no longer silently dropped\n- **Observability**: Late elements properly appear in configured side outputs\n- **Debugging**: No more mysterious data loss without explanation\n- **Compliance**: Ensures configured late data handling is actually executed\n\n### No Negative Impacts:\n- **Performance**: Exception only thrown in edge case scenario\n- **Compatibility**: No changes to API or normal processing logic\n- **Behavior**: Only adds handling for previously broken scenarios\n\n## Files Included\n\n```\n/workspace/\n├── flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/\n│   ├── WindowOperator.java (FIXED)\n│   └── EvictingWindowOperator.java (FIXED)\n├── SOLUTION_SUMMARY.md (this file)\n├── IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md (detailed implementation notes)\n└── BUGFIX_PLAN.md (original planning document)\n```\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThis fix ensures that late-arriving elements that could extend session windows are properly handled according to user configuration.
